result.markdown:
--- title: "104: Differences" date: "2024-08-16" categories: - gt - rvest format: html: code-fold: true code-summary: "Code" editor: visual --- ### Scrape power ratings ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(cbbplotR) # load in SP+ ratings # saved in CSV because they're behind a paywall at ESPN+ # https://www.espn.com/college-football/insider/story/_/id/40836337/college-football-2024-preseason-sp+-rankings-takeaways data <- readr::read_csv("sp.csv") # function to clean table and correct names # this is sort of sloppy and anti-dry (oh well) clean_sp_table <- function(sp) { sp |> dplyr::mutate(team = gsub("[^a-zA-Z]", "", Team), sp_rating = `SP+`, sp_off_rating = stringr::str_extract(`Off. SP+`, "\\d+\\.?\\d*"), sp_off_rk = stringr::str_extract(`Off. SP+`, pattern = "(?<=\\().*(?=\\))"), sp_def_rk = stringr::str_extract(`Def. SP+`, pattern = "(?<=\\().*(?=\\))"), sp_def_rating = stringr::str_extract(`Def. SP+`, "\\d+\\.?\\d*"), sp_st_rk = stringr::str_extract(`ST SP+`, pattern = "(?<=\\().*(?=\\))"), sp_st_rating = stringr::str_extract(`ST SP+`, "\\d+\\.?\\d*"), ) |> dplyr::mutate_at(c('sp_rating', 'sp_off_rating', 'sp_off_rk', 'sp_def_rk', 'sp_def_rating', 'sp_st_rk', 'sp_st_rating'), as.numeric) |> dplyr::mutate( # change names to be abbreviated team = dplyr::case_match(team, 'NCarolina' ~ 'North Carolina', 'OhioSt' ~ 'Ohio State', 'TexasAM' ~ 'Texas A&M', 'PennSt' ~ 'Penn State', 'IowaSt' ~ 'Iowa State', 'NotreDame' ~ 'Notre Dame', 'ArizonaSt' ~ 'Arizona State', 'OleMiss' ~ 'Ole Miss', 'MichiganSt' ~ 'Michigan State', 'NCSt' ~ 'NC State', 'MissSt' ~ 'Mississippi State', 'OklahomaSt' ~ 'Oklahoma State', 'KansasSt' ~ 'Kansas State', 'CoastalCaro' ~ 'Coastal Carolina', 'WakeForest' ~ 'Wake Forest', 'SanDiegoSt' ~ 'San Diego State', 'AppSt' ~ 'Appalachian State', 'BoiseSt' ~ 'Boise State', 'FloridaSt' ~ 'Florida State', 'FresnoSt' ~ 'Fresno State', 'VaTech' ~ 'Virginia Tech', "WVirginia" ~ "West Virginia", "TexasTech" ~ "Texas Tech", "BostonColl" ~ "Boston College", "JMU" ~ "James Madison", "WKU" ~ "Western Kentucky", "ECU" ~ "East Carolina", "OregonSt" ~ "Oregon State", "WashSt" ~ "Washington State", "AirForce" ~ "Air Force", "ODU" ~ "Old Dominion", 'GaTech' ~ 'Georgia Tech', "SCarolina" ~ "South Carolina", "SanJoseSt" ~ "San Jose State", "ColoradoSt" ~ "Colorado State", "FAU" ~ "Florida Atlantic", "MTSU" ~ "Middle Tennessee", "EMU" ~ "Eastern Michigan", "MiamiOH" ~ "Miami (OH)", "GeorgiaSt" ~ "Georgia State", "UtahSt" ~ "Utah State", "KentSt" ~ "Kent State", "BallSt" ~ "Ball State", "SAlabama" ~ "South Alabama", "NIU" ~ "Northern Illinois", "USF" ~ "South Florida", "LaTech" ~ "Louisiana Tech", "NTexas" ~ "North Texas", "GaSouthern" ~ "Georgia Southern", "NewMexico" ~ "New Mexico", "SoMiss" ~ "Southern Mississippi", "TexasSt" ~ "Texas State", "ArkansasSt" ~ "Arkansas State", "CMU" ~ "Central Michigan", "ULM" ~ "Louisiana Monroe", "BGSU" ~ "Bowling Green", "WMU" ~ "Western Michigan", "NMSU" ~ "New Mexico State", "ULMonroe" ~ "Louisiana Monroe", "Massachusetts" ~ "UMass", "FIU" ~ "Florida International", "KennesawSt" ~ "Kennesaw State", "SamHouston" ~ "Sam Houston State", "SJSU" ~ "San Jose State", "SDSU" ~ "San Diego State", "JvilleSt" ~ "Jacksonville State", team ~ team ) ) |> dplyr::select(team_name = team, sp_rating, sp_off_rk, sp_off_rating, sp_def_rk, sp_def_rating, sp_st_rk, sp_st_rating) |> dplyr::arrange(-sp_rating) |> dplyr::mutate(sp_rk = dplyr::row_number()) |> dplyr::relocate(sp_rk, .before = team_name) } sp <- clean_sp_table(sp = data) # Scape FEI ratings from the website # Also, going to try to get these in a more similar format # FEI shows median possessions per game is 26, so 13 per team # the avergage points per drive is 2.15 since 2007-2023 https://www.bcftoys.com/drives # so, we can take the fei_rating + 2.15 * 13 to get a similar number # is this correct, maybe? fei_url <- "https://www.bcftoys.com/2024-fei/" webpage <- rvest::read_html(fei_url) fei <- webpage |> rvest::html_node("table") |> rvest::html_table() |> dplyr::slice(-1) |> dplyr::select(fei_rk = X1, team_name = X2, fei_rating = X3, ofei = X5, ofei_rk = X6, dfei = X7, dfei_rk = X8, stfei = X9, stfei_rk = X10, EWins = X19) |> dplyr::slice(-1) |> dplyr::filter(team_name != "Team") |> na.omit() |> dplyr::mutate_at(dplyr::vars(-team_name), as.numeric) |> dplyr::mutate(team_name = dplyr::case_match(team_name, "Connecticut" ~ "UConn", "UL Monroe" ~ "Louisiana Monroe", "Sam Houston" ~ "Sam Houston State", "Massachusetts" ~ "UMass", team_name ~ team_name ), fei_adj = (2.15 + fei_rating) * 11.5) # Run anti-join to find the team_names that are NOT matching # commenting this out for now # fei |> # dplyr::anti_join(sp, by = "team_name") |> # dplyr::select(team_name) # A tibble: 10 × 1 # Scrape KFord Ratings from website # website is not behind paywall, but scraping the table was sub-optimal # grabbed it manually and saved via CSV # https://kfordratings.com/power kford <- readr::read_csv("kford.csv") |> dplyr::rename(kford_rk = Rank, team_name = Team, kford_rating = Rating) |> dplyr::mutate(team_name = dplyr::case_match(team_name, "Miami (FL)" ~ "Miami", "Pitt" ~ "Pittsburgh", "WKU" ~ "Western Kentucky", "Miami (Ohio)" ~ "Miami (OH)", "NIU" ~ "Northern Illinois", "USF" ~ "South Florida", "Sam Houston" ~ "Sam Houston State", "Southern Miss" ~ "Southern Mississippi", "ULM" ~ "Louisiana Monroe", "FIU" ~ "Florida International", team_name ~ team_name )) ``` ::: ### Combine and normalize the ratings ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # combine all ratings by joining on team_name trio <- kford |> dplyr::left_join(sp, by ="team_name") |> dplyr::left_join(fei, by = "team_name") |> dplyr::select(team_name, sp_rating, kford_rating, fei_rating, kford_rk, fei_rk, sp_rk) # normalize the ratings using a function norm <- function(x) { (x - mean(x)) / sd(x) } diff_rtg <- trio |> dplyr::mutate( sp_norm = norm(sp_rating), kford_norm = norm(kford_rating), fei_norm = norm(fei_rating) ) |> dplyr::rowwise() |> # run against each individual team dplyr::mutate( rating_range = max(kford_norm, fei_norm, sp_norm) - min(kford_norm, fei_norm, sp_norm), rating_sd = sd(c(kford_norm, fei_norm, sp_norm)), avg_rating = mean(c(kford_norm, fei_norm, sp_norm)) ) |> dplyr::ungroup() |> dplyr::select(team_name, rating_range, rating_sd, avg_rating, sp_rk, fei_rk, kford_rk, sp_rating, fei_rating, kford_rating) |> dplyr::arrange(-rating_range, -rating_sd) ``` ::: GT Theme ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} gt_theme_chris <- function(gt_object, ...) { stopifnot("'gt_object' must be a 'gt_tbl', have you accidentally passed raw data?" = "gt_tbl" %in% class(gt_object)) gt_object |> gt::tab_options( heading.align = "left", table.border.top.style = "none", column_labels.border.bottom.style = "none", column_labels.border.bottom.color = "black", table_body.border.top.style = "none", table_body.border.bottom.color = "white", heading.border.bottom.style = "none", data_row.padding = gt::px(5), table.font.size = gt::px(12), column_labels.font.size = gt::px(12), column_labels.font.weight = "bolder", column_labels.border.bottom.width = gt::px(1), ... ) |> gt::tab_style( style = gt::cell_text( color = "#333333", font = gt::google_font("Roboto"), transform = "uppercase" ), locations = list( gt::cells_column_labels(), gt::cells_stubhead() ) ) |> gt::tab_style( style = gt::cell_text( font = gt::google_font("Roboto"), weight = 800 ), locations = gt::cells_title(groups = "title") ) |> gt::tab_style( style = gt::cell_text( font = gt::google_font("Roboto"), weight = 400 ), locations = gt::cells_body() ) |> gt::tab_style( locations = gt::cells_title(groups = "subtitle"), style = gt::cell_text( size = "small" ) ) |> gt::tab_style( locations = gt::cells_title(groups = "title"), style = gt::cell_text( size = "large" ) ) |> gt::tab_style( locations = gt::cells_source_notes(), style = gt::cell_text( size = "x-small" ) ) |> gt::tab_style( style = gt::cell_borders(sides = "top", color = 'black', weight = gt::px(1.5), style = 'solid'), locations = gt::cells_body( rows = gt::everything() ) ) |> gt::tab_style( locations = gt::cells_row_groups(groups = gt::everything()), style = list( gt::cell_text( size = "small", weight = "bold" ), gt::cell_fill(color = "#FFFEE0") )) |> gt::tab_style( style = list( gt::cell_fill(color = "#FFFEE0"), gt::cell_text(color = "#333333", size = gt::px(12), weight = "bold"), gt::cell_borders(sides = "right", color = "black", weight = gt::px(2)), gt::cell_borders(sides = "bottom", color = "black", weight = gt::px(2)) ), locations = list( gt::cells_column_spanners( spanners = gt::everything() ) ) ) } ``` ::: ### GT Table with row groups ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # prepare the table by grabbing the widest range and slimmest range diff_rtg |> dplyr::arrange(-rating_range, -rating_sd) |> dplyr::slice(1:10) |> dplyr::mutate(range_group = "highest", color = "Reds") -> high_range diff_rtg |> dplyr::arrange(rating_range, rating_sd) |> dplyr::slice(1:10) |> dplyr::mutate(range_group = "lowest", color = "Greens") -> low_range diff_groups <- high_range |> dplyr::bind_rows(low_range) |> dplyr::mutate_at(dplyr::vars( rating_range, rating_sd, avg_rating, sp_rating, fei_rating, kford_rating ), ~ sprintf("%.2f", .)) |> dplyr::relocate(kford_rk, .before = fei_rk, fei_rating) |> dplyr::relocate(kford_rating, .before = fei_rating) disperse_tbl <- function(high_or_low, color) { table_header <- glue::glue( "
2024 Preseason FBS Power RatingsTeams with {high_or_low} dispersion Compares the preseason ratings across SP+, FEI, and KFORD.
" ) diff_groups |> dplyr::filter(range_group == high_or_low) |> dplyr::filter(color == color) |> cbbplotR::gt_cbb_teams(team_name, team_name) |> gt::gt() |> gt::fmt_markdown(team_name) |> gtExtras::gt_merge_stack( col1 = sp_rk, col2 = sp_rating, palette = c("black", "#333333") ) |> gtExtras::gt_merge_stack( col1 = kford_rk, col2 = kford_rating, palette = c("black", "#333333") ) |> gtExtras::gt_merge_stack( col1 = fei_rk, col2 = fei_rating, palette = c("black", "#333333") ) |> gt::cols_label( team_name = "", rating_range = "Range", rating_sd = "SD", avg_rating = "Avg", sp_rk = "SP+", kford_rk = "KFORD", fei_rk = "FEI" ) |> gt::data_color( columns = rating_range, direction = c("column"), palette = c(color), ) |> gt::data_color( columns = c(sp_rk, kford_rk, fei_rk), direction = c("column"), palette = c("YlGn"), reverse = TRUE, alpha = 0.4, ) |> gt::cols_hide(columns = c(range_group, color)) |> gt::tab_spanner(label = gt::html("Measure of
Dispersion"), columns = c(rating_range, rating_sd, avg_rating)) |> gt::tab_spanner(label = gt::html("Rank
Power Rating"), columns = c(sp_rk, kford_rk, fei_rk)) |> gt::cols_align(align = "left", columns = c(team_name)) |> gtExtras::gt_add_divider(columns = c(team_name, avg_rating), sides = "right", color = "black") |> gt::tab_header(title = gt::html(table_header)) |> gt::tab_source_note(source_note = gt::html("
Each system uses a different scale for its power ratings, so we're normalizing the ratings and comparing the range, standard deviation, and average rating. Highlights the raw ranking, so its faster to compare teams. Lists the power rating in each system's own scale underneath each rank.
Preseason ratings data sources
SP+, Bill Connelly, ESPN+
FEI, Brian Fremeau, bcftoys.com/
KFORD, Kelley Ford, kfordratings.com
Table by Chris (@dadgumboxscores) + Bless your chart | August 17, 2024")) |> cbbplotR::gt_theme_savant() |> gt::tab_style( style = gt::cell_borders(sides = "top", color = 'black', weight = gt::px(1.5), style = 'solid'), locations = gt::cells_body( rows = gt::everything() ) ) |> gt::tab_options( heading.align = "left", ) |> gt::tab_style( locations = gt::cells_row_groups(groups = gt::everything()), style = list( gt::cell_text( align = "center" ), gt::cell_borders(sides = c("top", "right"), color = "black", weight = gt::px(2)) )) } table_highest <- disperse_tbl("highest", "Reds") # For teams with smallest dispersion table_lowest <- disperse_tbl("lowest", "Greens") # Save the tables gtExtras::gtsave_extra(table_highest, filename = "highest_dispersion.png", vheight = 850, vwidth = 650) gtExtras::gtsave_extra(table_lowest, filename = "lowest_dispersion.png", vheight = 850, vwidth = 650) ``` :::
